summary: Market closed (Saturday). First run — no prior journal history. All four positions underwater; MSFT -12.3%, NVDA -11.3%, QQQ -4.9%, SPY -0.6%. SMA signals: only QQQ shows long, rest flat or deteriorating.
open_theses: MSFT: hold or reassess Monday — down 12.3% from entry at $425.31, SMA bearish (fast $377 < slow $406), needs fresh fundamental catalyst check before deciding to average down or trim
NVDA: hold or reassess Monday — down 11.3% from entry at $217.09, SMA bearish (fast $204 < slow $212), AI capex cycle narrative still intact but price action weak
QQQ: only position with SMA long signal (fast $724 > slow $722); still below entry at $742.88, but relative strength vs peers; monitor for recovery
SPY: nearly flat vs entry (-0.6%); broad market health indicator — SPY SMA flat, next open June 29 will reflect any weekend macro developments
Cash $853.46 available (~9.3% of equity); Monday research should check macro calendar (FOMC, jobs, CPI), sector rotation, and whether to deploy or protect
notes_for_monday: First-run session. No prior journal to compare against. Priorities: (1) research each position with fresh news/earnings check; (2) assess whether MSFT and NVDA drawdowns (-12%) are recoverable or need cuts; (3) check macro calendar for week of June 29; (4) consider whether to add to QQQ given its relative strength signal.
